metadata.content_note: Grade 6 English production SkillPackage with real guided mission and Skill Practice level banks.

subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Enter Vocabulary Observatory, where readers solve Grade 6 English challenges with careful reading and explanation.
lesson.objective: I can use context, roots, affixes, relationships, connotation, figurative language, and domain-specific vocabulary to determine word meanings.
lesson.concept_explanation: I can use context, roots, affixes, relationships, connotation, figurative language, and domain-specific vocabulary to determine word meanings. Use the text, language, punctuation, and context to prove your thinking.
lesson.common_misconception: A reader may ignore context clues, confuse connotation, treat figurative language literally, or use the everyday meaning of a domain word.
lesson.real_world_connection: Middle school readers use this skill in novels, science articles, history sources, directions, debates, and assessments.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read carefully, name the clue or evidence, explain how it supports the answer, and check that your response matches the whole text.
lesson.mini_lesson: Step 1: read the text or sentence. Step 2: mark the clue. Step 3: choose or write the answer. Step 4: explain why the clue proves it.

audio.text: context clues, academic vocabulary, root, prefix, suffix, synonym, antonym, connotation
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.prompt: Watch: In the sentence, what does analyze mean? The class will analyze the poem by studying its parts.

worked_examples.sentence: The class will analyze the poem by studying its parts.
worked_examples.explanation: study closely is supported by the clue in the text.

hint_ladder: Reread the sentence, passage, or prompt carefully.
Mark the clue: word, punctuation, phrase, context, or evidence.
Choose or write the answer and explain how the clue supports it.

guided_practice.explanation: A strong response includes work together.

misconception_bank.context_clue_ignored: context clue ignored; the learner needs a clue-based reread, model answer, and explanation check.
misconception_bank.domain_word_confusion: domain word confusion; the learner needs a clue-based reread, model answer, and explanation check.
misconception_bank.connotation_confusion: connotation confusion; the learner needs a clue-based reread, model answer, and explanation check.
misconception_bank.literal_vs_figurative_confusion: literal vs figurative confusion; the learner needs a clue-based reread, model answer, and explanation check.

game_theme.title: Vocabulary Observatory
game_theme.name: Vocabulary Observatory
game_theme.mission_intro: Practice academic vocabulary and figurative language through a mission built from the Grade 6 English completion plan.

story.text: Read This Page. Welcome to Vocabulary Observatory. In this Grade 6 English mission, you will use academic vocabulary and figurative language with real texts and questions. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: Read This Page. Learn the strategy, study the visual model, and use Listen when hearing a sentence, passage, vocabulary word, example, or evidence will help. Press Next after you can explain the steps.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Read This Page. Watch the model. Notice how the reader slows down, checks meaning, and proves each answer with the text. Use Listen to hear the model read clearly.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Read This Page. The demo walks through one example before you answer. Listen to the example, repeat useful language, and notice why the answer is supported.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Read This Page. Practice uses a guided question. Press Read Question before answering, use the hint ladder if you need help, and use Listen for sentence, passage, vocabulary, or evidence reading.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Read This Page. Challenge questions are a little closer and require careful reasoning. Press Read Question, listen when helpful, and choose or write the answer that best fits the text.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Read This Page. The checkpoint checks your independent understanding. Press Read Question for each item, use relevant evidence or examples, and avoid unsupported guesses.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Read This Page. You earned a badge for careful Grade 6 English work with academic vocabulary and figurative language. Press Next to see your profile and choose more practice.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Read This Page. Your profile saves accuracy, hints, stars, and misconception notes. Choose Practice This Skill to open the Skill Practice Center levels, or return to the Grade 6 hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
